description: This figure is a schematic diagram of Taylor factor M distribution along the tensile axis (TA) direction in the designed Fe-35Mn-6Al-0.1C low-carbon low-density cryogenic steel. The three vertices of the triangular diagram correspond to three crystallographic orientations: <001> orientation with M value of 2.30, <111> orientation with M value of 3.64, and <101> orientation with M value of 3.64. The diagram is divided into different regions: grains with M < 2.7, intermediate M region, and grains with M > 3.06, where regions with intermediate and high M are marked as "Twins", indicating that deformation twinning is preferentially activated in grains with higher Taylor factor. This schematic supports the conclusion that stacking fault energy is not the only decisive factor controlling deformation twinning in this steel, and twinning exhibits strong orientation dependence.
